selfCheck: Total kills cleared the guide's minimum comfortably: 24 chapter-level kills.
F5 and F7 both triggered; the scene endings were re-read specifically to avoid a false clean pass.
Hedge calls were kept to direct deletion-test survivors only; tactile uses of felt were not auto-killed.
Essay-brain calls were limited to clear thesis/evidence/restatement paragraphs rather than any merely clear paragraph.

findings.issue: Opening page keeps snapping from medium declaratives into clipped fragment captions.
examples: The wrongness ran under that polish.
No escort. No bow lane. No patience for pretending she could go over a wall like a ranger.

findings.issue: Estate-interior description repeats inventory sentence followed by fragment summary.
examples: Men moved through the yard with baskets, ledgers, harness, sacks. Workers. Actual workers, not prisoners shoved through an ugly theater.
Barov money. Barov order. Barov hands still feeding the machine.
Enchanted stones in the walls, then. Imported work. Old money.

findings.issue: Lake-crossing section relies on repeated short fragment pivots and emphatic one-line reversals.
examples: Preservation. Minerals. Old water kept too long in deep stone cisterns.
No ambush in the crossing. Worse than an ambush.

findings.issue: Archive-reading paragraphs repeat analytic fragment stacks instead of varying sentence shape.
examples: Host response, retention through storage, patterned answer in conditioned tissue. Incomplete, still. The final waking step absent.
References cut short. Samples transferred inward. Pressure readings taken beneath the archive foundation.

findings.issue: Final descent returns to the same clipped rhythm: setup sentence followed by caption fragments.
examples: The route down began behind a door disguised as paneling at the end of a service hall Jaina had not been shown earlier. Of course it did.
The walls narrowed. The treads had been worn smooth by centuries of feet before newer traffic laid its own path over the old wear.
No slam. No lock grinding into place. Just wood meeting stone with the finality of a decision already made elsewhere.

findings.line: His smile held.
findings.reason: The beat is only a stock facial-hold verb; it could belong to any controlled noble in the scene.

findings.line: Jandice's composure tightened. Enough to show the wall.
findings.reason: The beat names a generic tightening and then translates it instead of giving a character-specific physical tell.

findings.line: She could hear Sylvanas asking how long everything would take.
findings.deletionTest: Sylvanas asked how long everything would take.

findings.line: "Lady Proudmoore," he said, as though she had been invited for precisely this hour.
findings.deletionTest: "Lady Proudmoore," he said. She had been invited for precisely this hour.

findings.line: Rural patronage of scholarship is not a crime yet, though I admit the larger cities often behave as if it should be.
findings.deletionTest: Rural patronage of scholarship is not a crime yet, though I admit the larger cities often behave it should be.
findings.reason: The hedge softens a direct accusation the sentence is already making.

findings.line: The kind of woman she would have argued with for hours in a Dalaran archive and only afterward noticed the lamps had burned low.
findings.deletionTest: The kind of woman she would have argued with for hours in a Dalaran archive until the lamps had burned low.

findings.line: They already knew exactly what had entered.
findings.whatItRestates: The ward pressure adjusted, marked her, and passed the knowledge inward.

findings.line: Routine was the point. Nobody bothered to hide what they were doing.
findings.whatItRestates: Open doors, assistants copying notes, sorting reagents, and checking labeled vials against ledgers.

findings.line: The care in it felt worse than disorder.
findings.whatItRestates: Every table was scrubbed, every tag tied straight, every instrument back in its place.

findings.line: Being understood hit Jandice hard enough to show.
findings.whatItRestates: Jandice's eager reaction to Jaina accurately naming the model and its implications.

findings.issue: The interrogation with Alexei and Jandice turns into tea, banter, and a guided tour too quickly once Jaina proves she is worth talking to.
Jandice and Jaina move from ideological danger to collaborative mutual recognition with too little sustained hostility.

findings.reason: The paragraph opens on 'expensive stillness,' inventories the room, then closes on 'Imported work. Old money.'
Alexei's body description ends by summarizing him as 'A man used to signing orders and letting other people carry them out.'
The paragraph catalogs clues from the ledger in bullet-point rhythm before landing on 'master.'

findings.line: Easy extraction ends here, she thought.
Omitted route. Private access. Higher clearance than Jandice intended to admit.
findings.couldEndEarlierAt: Above, on the opposite wall, a gallery door stood shut under three separate sigils all feeding the same line of power downward.

findings.line: The next move belonged to someone else now.
Just wood meeting stone with the finality of a decision already made elsewhere.
findings.couldEndEarlierAt: Behind them, somewhere above, the panel door eased shut.

clusterFlags: smart-POV inference cluster around the Antonidas call
selfCheck: The single KEEP was re-tested against the closed keep list and survives as literal magical surveillance.
Candidate count stayed mostly KILL-heavy; the keep rate remained below the guide's leniency warning.
Analytical-POV sections produced repeated hits, so borderline cases in those clusters were not protected as 'voice.'

findings.line: Eastweald had the insolence to look healthy.
findings.subject: Eastweald
findings.realAgent: the maintained orchards, trimmed ditches, and visible labor made the land look healthy
findings.reason: Pattern 5 — abstraction/location given a human attitude.

findings.line: One corner of her mouth tried for a smile and thought better of it.
findings.subject: one corner of her mouth
findings.realAgent: Jandice almost smiled and then checked herself
findings.reason: Pattern 5 — body part gets desire and decision-making instead of the person.

findings.line: Jaina felt the watchful pressure adjust, mark her, then pass the knowledge inward.
findings.subject: watchful pressure
findings.verb: adjust / mark / pass the knowledge inward
findings.realAgent: the ward lattice sensed her entry and transmitted it deeper into the keep
findings.reason: Pattern 6/7 hybrid — magical atmosphere is made to sense and report like a watcher.

findings.line: I found a building using Kirin Tor bones to grow a second body around them.
findings.subject: building
findings.realAgent: the scholars inside are using Kirin Tor methods to expand a second research system
findings.reason: Pattern 6 — architecture is given knowledge-bearing, tool-using agency.

findings.line: The weight in the stone said more than Jandice's route did.
findings.subject: the weight in the stone
findings.realAgent: the intensified ward pressure and stressed masonry revealed more than the curated tour
findings.reason: Pattern 6 — scenery is made to speak instead of the observable evidence being named directly.

findings.line: Wards in the room listened for voices all the same.
findings.subject: wards
findings.realAgent: the ward spell was literally tuned to vibration and voice
findings.reason: Literal non-human action — the line describes actual magical surveillance mechanics.

findings.line: Page after page drove toward the same answer.
findings.subject: page after page
findings.realAgent: the accumulated notes and experimental results pushed Jaina toward the same conclusion
findings.reason: Pattern 2 — written evidence is framed as actively pressing an answer.

findings.line: The answering silence carried relief and anger braided too tightly to separate.
findings.subject: silence
findings.realAgent: Antonidas's pause, the broken connection, and his strained voice conveyed relief and anger
findings.reason: Pattern 7 — silence is made to deliver emotional content.

findings.line: If the building is what you think it is, it has already told its master you are there.
findings.subject: building
findings.realAgent: its wards and reporting mechanisms already alerted the master
findings.reason: Pattern 6 — architecture is given awareness and communication.

findings.line: The Council saw the gate and declared the field forbidden so they would never have to learn what lay beyond it.
findings.subject: The Council
findings.realAgent: specific councilors and archmages forbade the field rather than confronting its implications
findings.reason: Pattern 4 — institution used as a self-protecting abstraction instead of naming the people hiding behind it.

audits.registerSummary: Jaina thinks like a tactician-scholar under pressure: wards, routes, leverage, notation, and bodies in space. Her internal language is sharp and analytical, but it stays practical and evidence-led. She would not naturally caption moments with epigraph-like fragments or authorial summaries of what the scene means.
audits.structuralHabit: yes — fragment captions recur at scene turns and emotional pivots
selfCheck: POV register was established before Tier B calls; Jaina's practical analytic voice provided the baseline.
Tier A kills were easy to find once scene transitions were re-read for caption fragments.
Child-guide overlaps were avoided where possible; this pass focused on residual captioning and register drift.

findings.line: That was the first true break in him. Tiny. Brief.
findings.povMismatch: The clipped verdict fragments read like an external caption pinned onto Alexei rather than Jaina's live tactical read.

findings.line: He did not flinch. Better than the guard.
findings.povMismatch: The second sentence is a caption fragment judging the beat from above.

findings.line: It was excellent. Of course it was.
findings.povMismatch: The follow-up fragment is a polished authorial shrug, not Jaina's in-scene sensory thinking.

findings.line: Antonidas's seminar marks. His school, carried forward in another hand.
findings.povMismatch: This lands as a slogan stack about lineage rather than an embodied recognition beat.

findings.line: Different hands, same lineage. The same training sat in every stroke.
findings.povMismatch: The fragment pair compresses the discovery into a polished thesis instead of staying with what she is seeing on the page.

findings.line: The story had been in the chalk all along.
findings.povMismatch: Jaina can infer provenance, but 'the story' is thematic hindsight rather than her immediate evidence-led thought.

findings.line: Enough to show the wall.
findings.povMismatch: This is a caption fragment translating Jandice's reaction after the physical beat already landed.

findings.line: Omitted route. Private access. Higher clearance than Jandice intended to admit.
findings.povMismatch: The three clipped labels read like dossier headings, not live thought inside Jaina's body.

findings.line: Being understood hit Jandice hard enough to show.
findings.povMismatch: The sentence compresses Jandice's reaction into a polished abstraction instead of the physical evidence Jaina would actually register.

findings.line: There should have been triumph in that. Instead her stomach tightened. The next move belonged to someone else now.
findings.povMismatch: The passage comments on the scene's significance and control shift like retrospective narration rather than immediate tactical fear.

findings.line: Of course it did.
findings.povMismatch: This one-line shrug is a caption beat, not a grounded thought attached to a specific sensory cue.

findings.line: Just wood meeting stone with the finality of a decision already made elsewhere.
findings.povMismatch: The sentence is too polished and thematic for Jaina's immediate descent-state register; it sounds written over the moment.

clusterWarnings: Pattern 1 direct-contrast pivots recur in characterization and setting description.
Late-scene pattern 5/8 pivots return for emphasis in the descent close.
selfCheck: Both KEEP verdicts survived the guide's structural tests: one binary diagnostic, one hard constraint.
The KEEP rate stayed at the upper limit but not above it; no aesthetic 'this sounds good' keeps were allowed.
Weak absence-reporting cases were mostly excluded unless a positive second beat clearly carried the sentence.

findings.line: Actual workers, not prisoners shoved through an ugly theater.
findings.reason: Pattern 1 — direct contrast pivot; the sentence lands on the positive noun and the negative half is scaffolding.

findings.line: He did not flinch. Better than the guard.
findings.reason: Pattern 5 — preamble negation feeding a caption fragment.

findings.line: Small burns from handling reagents in glass, not reading about them from a safe chair.
findings.deletionTest: Small burns from handling reagents in glass.
findings.reason: Pattern 1 — the negative comparison delays the real image.

findings.line: "That is their burden, not ours."
findings.reason: Pattern 1 — direct contrast pivot used for rhetorical neatness rather than a concrete decision split.

findings.line: A notice, not a wall.
findings.reason: Binary diagnostic — the distinction between warning and barrier changes Jaina's immediate decision to proceed.

findings.line: Students did not cluster on the landing. A single porter in dark clothes waited with a lantern hooded against daylight.
findings.deletionTest: A single porter in dark clothes waited with a lantern hooded against daylight.
findings.reason: Pattern 5 — the real information is the porter; the negated crowd is setup.

findings.line: Jaina did not look up. "I see disciplined work."
findings.reason: Pattern 5 — the sentence pair primes the line with negated staging before the real beat arrives.

findings.line: He would be right in every way that did not solve the problem fast enough.
findings.deletionTest: He would be right in every way.
findings.reason: Pattern 1 — the negative tail supplies the actual force; the sentence improves when it commits directly.

findings.line: She set a muting line round the desk with the smallest ward she knew, one meant for privacy between scholars in crowded archive rooms, not for this depth of interference.
findings.deletionTest: She set a muting line round the desk with the smallest ward she knew, one meant for privacy between scholars in crowded archive rooms.
findings.reason: Hard constraint — the negation states the spell's actual capability limit, which affects the risk calculus.

findings.line: No slam. No lock grinding into place. Just wood meeting stone with the finality of a decision already made elsewhere.
findings.deletionTest: Just wood meeting stone with the finality of a decision already made elsewhere.
findings.reason: Pattern 8 — mundane reveal chain; the sentence builds drama by rejecting ordinary sounds before naming the real beat.

summary.verdictReason: 53 KILL verdicts across 4 guides. Strongest recurring problems: GPT-5 F1 fragment rhythm, narrator-seep caption fragments, intent smear onto buildings/silence/institutions, and negation pivots used for emphasis.
